description_long: This object is a vintage medium format camera, reminiscent of classic Hasselblad models, featuring a boxy, metallic body with a deep blue finish and gold trim. The camera has a prominent lens assembly at the front, surrounded by textured focus and aperture rings, and a waist-level viewfinder that pops up from the top. On the sides, there are various dials, levers, and a film advance crank, all crafted with metallic detailing. The back of the camera is flat with a rectangular film door and a latch, while the top viewfinder hood is ribbed and angular. The overall construction suggests a robust, professional-grade device designed for high-quality photography. The camera's design is both functional and decorative, with a blend of utilitarian controls and elegant accents.
description: A vintage, boxy medium format camera with a blue and gold metallic body, prominent lens, and pop-up waist-level viewfinder. It features detailed dials, levers, and a classic, robust design.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the flat back panel with a rectangular film door and latch, as well as the raised waist-level viewfinder.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the film advance crank, a large control dial, and the metallic trim along the camera's edge.
description_view_2: The front view displays the prominent lens assembly, textured focus and aperture rings, and the open waist-level viewfinder above.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals additional control dials, a circular port, and the continuation of the gold trim and blue body.
